events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: hot work over sewer/drain, in progress. NFPA 51B prohibits hot work over drains/sewers unless protected. Need to stop work, evaluate, protect drains/sewers, obtain proper permit authorization. Sequence violation: hot work should not proceed over sewer/drain without fire watch, spark shielding, covering drains, permit review. Decision fail, risk critical.

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work actively in progress over an unprotected sewer/drain",
    "No evidence of drain/sewer covering or spark containment",
    "Permit conditions for hot work near sewer/drain not satisfied before work began"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop the hot work",
    "Remove ignition sources from the area",
    "Cover/seal all floor drains, sewer openings, and catch basins with noncombustible material",
    "Verify the area is free of flammable/combustible vapors and liquids",
    "Assign a dedicated fire watch with extinguishing equipment",
    "Re-inspect and revalidate the hot work permit before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J require hot work areas to be free of combustibles and protected from sparks/slag reaching drains, sewers, or other openings where flammable vapors may accumulate. Performing hot work over an unprotected sewer/drain is a critical sequence and permit violation; work must stop until the openings are protected and the permit is revalidated.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B - Standard for Fire Prevention During Welding, Cutting, and Other Hot Work",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart J - Welding and Cutting"
  ]
}
